message:
perf(search): preallocate rrf_fuse_weighted score table (~20% faster, p<0.05)

rrf_fuse_weighted constructed an empty `HashMap<u64, (f32, _, _)>` with
the default capacity (0) and then inserted up to `|lexical| + |semantic|`
distinct keys, forcing a chain of rehashes as the map grew. The inner
scoring loops also recomputed `k as f32` on every iteration and called
into a helper that re-checked `weight <= 0.0` per item.

Changes (frankenterm-core/src/search/hybrid_search.rs):

  * HashMap::with_capacity(lexical.len() + semantic.len()) — upper bound
    on distinct keys, eliminates all rehash work in the hot path.
  * Hoist `let k_f = k as f32;` once per call so the two loops do
    `k_f + rank as f32 + 1.0` instead of re-casting on every iteration.
  * Split each lane's loop on `weight > 0.0` so the zero-weight branch
    skips the division entirely while still recording `lexical_rank` /
    `semantic_rank` for downstream callers that rely on them.
  * Delete the now-unused `rrf_component_score` helper (compiler flagged
    it dead after the weight/zero split was inlined).

Criterion measurements (frankensearch_bench::rrf_fusion, --measurement-time
5, /tmp/ft-pane6-target, bench profile, criterion's own stat-sig deltas):

  equal_size/10          2.26 µs → 1.16 µs   change: -40.82% (p < 0.05)
  equal_size/50          6.87 µs → 5.79 µs   change: -20.76% (p < 0.05)
  equal_size/100        13.52 µs → 11.14 µs  change: -17.02% (p < 0.05)
  equal_size/500        82.66 µs → 67.04 µs  change: -19.34% (p < 0.05)
  equal_size/1000      175.61 µs → 141.79 µs change: -21.29% (p < 0.05)
  asymmetric_1000x10   135.37 µs → 103.40 µs change: -20.45% (p < 0.05)

All six bench cases regress in p<0.05 territory. Behavior is unchanged:
the scoring formula and sort order are identical, and the zero-weight
branch still records the rank the tests depend on.
